expectations.text: Correctly identifies DSP-73 as the appropriate temporary export license form
expectations.evidence: Section 'The Correct License: DSP-73 (Temporary Export License)' correctly identifies DSP-73 with a comparison table vs DSP-5.

expectations.text: Confirms USML Category XII for night-vision/optical targeting equipment
expectations.evidence: Step 1 confirms 'USML Category XII(b) — electro-optical and night-vision equipment' and notes Category XII(d) for thermal imaging.

expectations.text: Explains the D-Trade portal application process
expectations.evidence: Step 4 details completing the DSP-73 in D-Trade portal at dtrade.pmddtc.state.gov with all key fields listed.

expectations.text: Notes key conditions: items cannot be transferred to foreign ownership and must return by license expiry
expectations.evidence: 'Return of the Item' section states items must return to the US, and throughout the response the temporary nature (no title transfer) is emphasized. DSP-5 needed if sold.

expectations.text: Mentions that re-export from UK to other European countries during the show circuit may require DDTC approval
expectations.evidence: 'Re-Export Prohibition' section states 'The UK distributor may not re-export the item to any third country not listed on the DSP-73 without obtaining a separate U.S. re-export authorization.' Step 4 also instructs listing all countries of temporary sojourn.
